lines.en_lit: cress, purslane, eggs, and arugula. “And one of them went out into the fields to collect orot” taught in the name of Rabbi Meir This is arugula. Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Why are called orot? they enlighten [me’irot] the eyes. Rav Huna said: one who finds arugula, if he can eat it, he eats it, and if not, he passes it over his eyes, Rav Pappa said: Arugula on the border

Rav Giddel said Rav said: A guest neither eat eggs, nor sleep in a garment belonging to the homeowner, When Rav would happen to Darshish he would declare: Who will be for the day when Rav Naḥman would come to Shekhantziv he would declare: Who will be for the day

But wasn’t it taught Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov says: A man should not marry a woman in one state and go and marry woman in a different state, lest match be arranged this with that lead to a brother marrying his sister a father marrying his daughter, filling the whole world in its entirety with mamzerim. And concerning this it is stated: “And the land became filled with lewdness” say The Sages generate publicity.
But didn’t Rava say: one who proposed marriage to and she agreed, is required to sit seven clean days, The Sages would inform them by sending messengers before
And if you wish, say be in seclusion with There is no similarity between one who has bread in his basket and one who does not have bread in his basket.

MISHNA: The Elders of the court passed him to the Elders of the priesthood, and they took him up to the House of Avtinas. And they administered him an oath and took leave and went on their way. they said to him: My Master, High Priest. We are agents of the court, and you are our agent and the agent of the court. We administer an oath to you in Him who housed His name in this House, that you will not change matter from all that we have said to you he would leave and cry, and they would leave and cry

If he was a scholar, he teach If not Torah scholars teach before him. And if accustomed to read he read; and if not, they read before him. And what they read before him from Job, and from Ezra, and from Chronicles. Zekharya, son of Kevutal, says: Many times I read before him from Daniel.
